steps.title: Notice the contrast in Jane
steps.content: Jane says she goes about her day's business "tranquilly," but thoughts of leaving Thornfield keep wandering through her mind. The passage contrasts her calm outward routine with restless inner thoughts that she does not fully check.

steps.title: Reject stronger claims
steps.content: B mentions loyalty, though Jane is imagining leaving. C invents deep fulfillment. D overstates her resolve; she frames advertisements involuntarily and says the thoughts might "germinate," not that she has decided. The answer is A.
